title: jovibarba globifera subsp. hirta
intro: Jovibarba globifera subsp. hirta, also known as the hairy Jovibarba, is a subspecies of the Jovibarba globifera plant, a type of flowering plant in the heath family (Ericaceae). Jovibarba globifera subsp. hirta is a low-growing, evergreen perennial with small white flowers and hairy leaves. The plant has been used in traditional herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which can be used to treat various health conditions such as wounds, infections, and respiratory issues. The herbal preparations of Jovibarba globifera subsp. hirta include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which can be used topically or internally to take advantage of its health benefits.
